title: JDBC Sink Connector Write Flow
interaction_flow: JdbcSinkConnectorTask → JdbcSinkConnectorConfig: new JdbcSinkConnectorConfig(props) [validates insertMode, deleteEnabled, batchSize, etc.]
JdbcSinkConnectorTask → createRecordWriter(): returns UnnestRecordWriter (if postgresUnnestInsert) or DefaultRecordWriter
JdbcSinkConnectorTask → JdbcChangeEventSink: new(config, session, dialect, recordWriter, connectorContext)
JdbcChangeEventSink → JdbcKafkaSinkRecord: new JdbcKafkaSinkRecord(kafkaSinkRecord, config) [for each record]
→ RecordWriter: writeTruncate(collectionId) [native SQL: TRUNCATE TABLE, commit]
JdbcChangeEventSink: [if isDelete() or isTombstone() and deleteEnabled]
→ [if upsert buffer non-empty: flush or remove from upsertBuffer]
→ [if delete buffer non-empty: flush or remove from deleteBuffer]
→ DatabaseDialect: resolveMissingFields(record, tableDescriptor) [if schema changed → flushBuffer + recreate buffer]
→ Buffer: add(record) → List<JdbcSinkRecord> toFlush [returns non-empty when batchSize reached]
JdbcChangeEventSink: after all records → flushBuffers(upsertBufferByTable) + flushBuffers(deleteBufferByTable)
[if INSERT]  → DatabaseDialect: getBatchInsertStatement() or getInsertStatement(table, record)
[if UPSERT]  → DatabaseDialect: getBatchUpsertStatement() or getUpsertStatement(table, record)
RecordWriter → Transaction: commit() [or rollback() on exception]
key_relationships: JdbcChangeEventSink implements ChangeEventSink, orchestrates entire write pipeline
DefaultRecordWriter implements RecordWriter, uses Hibernate StatelessSession + JDBC PreparedStatement
DatabaseDialect provides SQL generation + schema inspection (tableExists, readTable, resolveMissingFields)
Buffer (RecordBuffer or ReducedRecordBuffer) accumulates records up to batchSize before flush
ReducedRecordBuffer consolidates multiple events for same PK into latest state
JdbcSinkConnectorConfig implements SinkConnectorConfig, holds InsertMode/SchemaEvolutionMode enums

insert_modes.INSERT: Standard SQL INSERT; bind key fields then non-key fields
insert_modes.UPSERT: Dialect-specific upsert (e.g. ON CONFLICT); requires primaryKeyMode + primaryKeyFields
insert_modes.UPDATE: Standard SQL UPDATE; bind non-key fields first, then key fields as WHERE clause

schema_evolution_modes.NONE: No DDL; table must already exist with correct schema
schema_evolution_modes.BASIC: Auto-CREATE table on first event; ALTER TABLE to add new columns as they appear

buffer_logic: Two buffer maps per execution batch: upsertBufferByTable (inserts/updates) and deleteBufferByTable (deletes). When a DELETE arrives: flush upsert buffer for that table first. When an INSERT arrives: flush delete buffer for that table first. This ordering prevents data inconsistency.
retry_logic: executeWithRetries() wraps flush calls. Up to flushMaxRetries (default 5) attempts with flushRetryDelayMs (default 1000ms) between tries. Retries only on retriable exceptions (communication failures per dialect.getCommunicationExceptions()).
test_pipeline_setup: AbstractJdbcSinkPipelineIT extends AbstractJdbcSinkIT. Tests use @TestTemplate + SourcePipelineInvocationContextProvider to run each test against multiple source/sink DB combos. startSink() starts Kafka Connect sink connector + Kafka consumer thread that populates consumerRecords queue. assertDataType() inserts to source DB, waits for CDC events in Kafka, verifies sink DB contents.
important_notes: JdbcSinkConnectorTask tracks offsets per TopicPartition; markProcessed() advances offset+1, markNotProcessed() rewinds on failure
UnnestRecordWriter (PostgreSQL optimization): single INSERT ... SELECT unnest(...) instead of per-record JDBC batching; 5-10x speedup
Schema change records (DDL events) are explicitly ignored with a warning
If schema detects missing fields mid-buffer: current buffer is flushed, then buffer recreated for new schema
connectionRestartOnErrors: if true, connector may restart on connection errors (risks data loss on async replicas)
HibernateSession used as StatelessSession (no L1 cache), direct JDBC work via doWork()
